sourceMovie.title: Upgrade
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Upgrade (2018) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Leigh Whannell's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Upgrade clicked for you, The Terminator (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Upgrade clicked for you, Looper (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Upgrade?
faq.answer: Try The Terminator, Looper, Repo Men—each shares DNA with Upgrade while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Upgrade?
faq.answer: Upgrade (2018) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Upgrade worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
